title: Next chapter: Restructuring GitHub’s bug bounty program
excerpt: GitHub is making some significant changes to its bug bounty program, shifting its focus to give researchers a better experience.
content_html:
GitHub is making some significant changes to its bug bounty program, shifting its focus to give researchers a better experience working with the GitHub team.

July 22, 2026

|

4 minutes

  • Share:

The security research community makes GitHub safer for everyone. That’s the simple idea behind our bug bounty program.

For more than a decade, researchers from around the world have helped us find and fix vulnerabilities before they could be exploited, and we’ve worked hard to be a program worth their time.

Today, we’re sharing some meaningful changes to how the program works. These decisions comes after months of reflecting on our program, analyzing what’s happening across the industry, and thinking about researcher experience.

What’s changed and why

The program is facing an increasing queue. We have already made adjustments to accommodate the rise in new researchers and the acceleration in efforts of researchers we’ve been working with. We shared these changes in a recent blog post.

These changes are about two things: reducing the noise so we can focus on the signal, and building a program that serious researchers find rewarding to participate in.

Introducing a permanent VIP program

We’re formalizing a permanent private/invite-only VIP program for qualified researchers who consistently deliver high-quality, high-impact work. VIP researchers get higher payouts, faster response times, and a closer working relationship with our security engineering team. The goal is to create a space where the researchers who invest deeply in understanding GitHub can work with us directly and get an experience that reflects the effort they put in.

VIP program bounty table:

How to qualify: We’ll publish clear criteria on our public HackerOne page. The path in is built around demonstrated, consistent quality. To qualify, you must accomplish at least one of the following:

  • One critical finding
  • Two high findings
  • Four medium findings
  • Seven low findings

The core shift here is in what we’re incentivizing: you don’t earn more by submitting more. You earn more by submitting better.

A restructured public bounty table

To commit ourselves to the changes in our prioritization above, we also must make changes that enable it. We are adjusting our public program rates to accommodate this shift in focus towards quality of relationships and findings over quantity of reports. We are also updating to static payouts—a single, clear number per severity level, rather than a wide range. Ranges sound flexible, but in practice they create uncertainty for researchers and overhead for our team. Static payouts set clear expectations on both sides, and we retain the ability to award discretionary bonuses for work that goes above and beyond.

Our new public program bounty table:

This adjustment will enable us to provide more tailored attention and higher rewards to our VIP program, while still enabling our public program to be a place to explore and serve as a feeder into the VIP program.

Raising the signal requirement

To reduce the volume of low-effort and AI-generated reports, we’re implementing a HackerOne signal requirement on the public program. Researchers who don’t yet meet the signal threshold will have a limited number of allowed submissions while they establish a track record.

This isn’t a wall against new researchers. HackerOne’s platform gives researchers who don’t meet the threshold up to four initial submissions, which is enough runway for a newcomer with a genuine finding to demonstrate their skills. We want to remain accessible to the full security research community; we just need a baseline that keeps the program workable for everyone.

What stays the same

Our commitment to rewarding real security research isn’t changing. We’ll continue to pay out quickly, communicate clearly, and treat researchers as the partners they are.

Reports submitted before these changes take effect will be honored under the previous bounty structure. We’re grandfathering the backlog so that only reports made on or after July 27, 2026 will be assessed with the new structure.

Looking ahead

This is one part of the broader evolution we’re working through. Alongside the bounty restructuring and the VIP program, we’re investing in faster response times, clearer severity reasoning, and more community engagement. Great working relationships are built on more than a pay table. You can engage directly with us at conferences like DEFCON and you’ll hear from us through ongoing outreach. We look forward to joining the researcher community at security conferences, building relationships, and continuing to explore ways to make our bug bounty program one that rewards the kind of deep, thoughtful research we care about most.

The security research community is one of GitHub’s greatest assets. These changes are meant to honor them. We want to build a program that attracts the research we value, creates an experience that reflects how seriously we take this work, and upholds the trust researchers place in us every time they submit a report.

We’ll see you out there, and happy hacking!

Written by

Product Security Engineer

Related posts

How GitHub gave every repository a durable owner

GitHub had over 14,000 repositories. Fewer than half had clear ownership. Here’s how we gave every active repository a validated owner in under 45 days, archived the rest, and made ownership the foundation for everything that followed.

We do newsletters, too

Discover tips, technical guides, and best practices in our biweekly newsletter just for devs.

VIP program bounty table: Severity Payout Low $1,000 Medium $7,500 High $20,000 Critical $30,000+ How to qualify: We’ll publish clear criteria on our public HackerOne page. The path in is built around demonstrated, consistent quality. Reports submitted before these changes take effect will be honored under the previous bounty structure. We’re grandfathering the backlog so that only reports made on or after July 27, 2026 will be assessed with the new structure. The security research community is one of GitHub’s greatest assets. These changes are meant to honor them. sentences.zh: 4分钟

blocks.html_en: Today, we’re sharing some meaningful changes to how the program works. These decisions comes after months of reflecting on our program, analyzing what’s happening across the industry, and thinking about researcher experience.

sentences.en: Today, we’re sharing some meaningful changes to how the program works.
sentences.zh: 今天，我们将分享该计划运作方式的一些有意义的变化。

sentences.en: These decisions comes after months of reflecting on our program, analyzing what’s happening across the industry, and thinking about researcher experience.
sentences.zh: 这些决定是在对我们的计划进行数月反思、分析整个行业正在发生的事情并思考研究人员经验之后做出的。

sentences.en: What’s changed and why
sentences.zh: 发生了什么变化以及原因

blocks.html_en: The program is facing an increasing queue. We have already made adjustments to accommodate the rise in new researchers and the acceleration in efforts of researchers we’ve been working with. We shared these changes in a recent blog post.

sentences.zh: 该计划面临着越来越多的队列。

sentences.en: We have already made adjustments to accommodate the rise in new researchers and the acceleration in efforts of researchers we’ve been working with.
sentences.zh: 我们已经做出了调整，以适应新研究人员的增加以及与我们合作的研究人员工作的加速。

sentences.en: We shared these changes in a recent blog post.
sentences.zh: 我们在最近的一篇博客文章中分享了这些变化。

sentences.en: These changes are about two things: reducing the noise so we can focus on the signal, and building a program that serious researchers find rewarding to participate in.
sentences.zh: 这些变化与两件事有关：减少噪音，以便我们可以专注于信号，以及建立一个让认真的研究人员发现参与有益的计划。

sentences.en: Introducing a permanent VIP program
sentences.zh: 推出永久 VIP 计划

blocks.html_en: We’re formalizing a permanent private/invite-only VIP program for qualified researchers who consistently deliver high-quality, high-impact work. VIP researchers get higher payouts, faster response times, and a closer working relationship with our security engineering team. The goal is to create a space where the researchers who invest deeply in understanding GitHub can work with us directly and get an experience that reflects the effort they put in.

sentences.en: We’re formalizing a permanent private/invite-only VIP program for qualified researchers who consistently deliver high-quality, high-impact work.
sentences.zh: 我们正在为那些始终如一地提供高质量、高影响力工作的合格研究人员制定一项永久私人/仅限邀请的 VIP 计划。

sentences.en: VIP researchers get higher payouts, faster response times, and a closer working relationship with our security engineering team.
sentences.zh: VIP 研究人员可以获得更高的报酬、更快的响应时间，并与我们的安全工程团队建立更紧密的工作关系。

sentences.en: The goal is to create a space where the researchers who invest deeply in understanding GitHub can work with us directly and get an experience that reflects the effort they put in.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标是创建一个空间，让那些深入了解 GitHub 的研究人员可以直接与我们合作，并获得反映他们所付出努力的体验。

sentences.zh: 这里的核心转变在于我们所激励的内容：您不会通过提交更多内容来赚取更多收入。

sentences.en: You earn more by submitting better.
sentences.zh: 通过更好地提交，您可以获得更多收入。

sentences.zh: 重组的公共赏金表

Static payouts set clear expectations on both sides, and we retain the ability to award discretionary bonuses for work that goes above and beyond.

sentences.zh: 我们还更新了静态支出——每个严重级别都有一个明确的数字，而不是一个很大的范围。

sentences.en: Ranges sound flexible, but in practice they create uncertainty for researchers and overhead for our team.
sentences.zh: 范围听起来很灵活，但实际上它们给研究人员带来了不确定性，并给我们的团队带来了开销。

sentences.en: Static payouts set clear expectations on both sides, and we retain the ability to award discretionary bonuses for work that goes above and beyond.
sentences.zh: 静态薪酬为双方设定了明确的期望，并且我们保留为超出工作范围的工作酌情颁发奖金的能力。

sentences.zh: 这一调整将使我们能够为我们的 VIP 计划提供更有针对性的关注和更高的奖励，同时仍然使我们的公共计划成为探索和服务 VIP 计划的一个渠道。

sentences.en: Raising the signal requirement
sentences.zh: 提高信号要求

blocks.html_en: To reduce the volume of low-effort and AI-generated reports, we’re implementing a HackerOne signal requirement on the public program. Researchers who don’t yet meet the signal threshold will have a limited number of allowed submissions while they establish a track record.

sentences.en: To reduce the volume of low-effort and AI-generated reports, we’re implementing a HackerOne signal requirement on the public program.
sentences.zh: 为了减少人工智能生成的省力报告的数量，我们正在公共计划上实施 HackerOne 信号要求。

sentences.en: Researchers who don’t yet meet the signal threshold will have a limited number of allowed submissions while they establish a track record.
sentences.zh: 尚未达到信号阈值的研究人员在建立跟踪记录时将获得有限数量的允许提交。

blocks.html_en: This isn’t a wall against new researchers. sentences.en: This isn’t a wall against new researchers.
sentences.zh: 这并不是阻挡新研究人员的墙。

sentences.en: HackerOne’s platform gives researchers who don’t meet the threshold up to four initial submissions, which is enough runway for a newcomer with a genuine finding to demonstrate their skills.
sentences.zh: HackerOne 的平台为未达到门槛的研究人员提供了最多四份初始提交材料，这对于具有真正发现的新人来说有足够的跑道来展示他们的技能。

sentences.en: We want to remain accessible to the full security research community; we just need a baseline that keeps the program workable for everyone.
sentences.zh: 我们希望继续与整个安全研究社区保持联系；我们只需要一个基线，使该计划对每个人都可行。

sentences.en: What stays the same
sentences.zh: 什么保持不变

blocks.html_en: Our commitment to rewarding real security research isn’t changing. We’ll continue to pay out quickly, communicate clearly, and treat researchers as the partners they are.

sentences.en: Our commitment to rewarding real security research isn’t changing.

sentences.en: We’ll continue to pay out quickly, communicate clearly, and treat researchers as the partners they are.
sentences.zh: 我们将继续快速付款，清晰沟通，并将研究人员视为合作伙伴。

sentences.zh: 在这些更改生效之前提交的报告将按照之前的赏金结构进行兑现。

sentences.en: We’re grandfathering the backlog so that only reports made on or after July 27, 2026 will be assessed with the new structure.
sentences.zh: 我们不再考虑积压工作，以便只有 2026 年 7 月 27 日或之后提交的报告才会使用新结构进行评估。

sentences.en: Looking ahead
sentences.zh: 展望未来

sentences.en: We look forward to joining the researcher community at security conferences, building relationships, and continuing to explore ways to make our bug bounty program one that rewards the kind of deep, thoughtful research we care about most.
sentences.zh: 我们期待在安全会议上加入研究人员社区，建立关系，并继续探索如何使我们的错误赏金计划成为奖励我们最关心的那种深入、深思熟虑的研究的方法。

We want to build a program that attracts the research we value, creates an experience that reflects how seriously we take this work, and upholds the trust researchers place in us every time they submit a report.

安全研究社区让 GitHub 对每个人来说都更安全。 这就是我们的错误赏金计划背后的简单想法。

十多年来，来自世界各地的研究人员帮助我们发现并修复漏洞，防止它们被利用，我们也一直努力成为一个值得他们花时间的项目。

今天，我们将分享该计划运作方式的一些有意义的变化。 这些决定是在对我们的计划进行数月反思、分析整个行业正在发生的事情并思考研究人员经验之后做出的。

发生了什么变化以及原因

该计划面临着越来越多的队列。 我们已经做出了调整，以适应新研究人员的增加以及与我们合作的研究人员工作的加速。 我们在最近的一篇博客文章中分享了这些变化。

这些变化与两件事有关：减少噪音，以便我们可以专注于信号，以及建立一个让认真的研究人员发现参与有益的计划。

推出永久 VIP 计划

我们正在为那些始终如一地提供高质量、高影响力工作的合格研究人员制定一项永久私人/仅限邀请的 VIP 计划。 VIP 研究人员可以获得更高的报酬、更快的响应时间，并与我们的安全工程团队建立更紧密的工作关系。 我们的目标是创建一个空间，让那些深入了解 GitHub 的研究人员可以直接与我们合作，并获得反映他们所付出努力的体验。

VIP计划奖金表：

如何获得资格：我们将在我们的 HackerOne 公共页面上发布明确的标准。 这条道路是围绕经过验证的、一致的质量而建立的。 要获得资格，您必须至少完成以下一项：

  • 一项重要发现
  • 两项高发现
  • 四种中等发现
  • 七项低发现

这里的核心转变在于我们所激励的内容：您不会通过提交更多内容来赚取更多收入。 通过更好地提交，您可以获得更多收入。

重组的公共赏金表

为了致力于改变上述优先顺序，我们还必须做出改变以实现这一目标。 我们正在调整我们的公共计划费率，以适应这种将重点转向关系质量和调查结果而不是报告数量的转变。 我们还更新了静态支出——每个严重级别都有一个明确的数字，而不是一个很大的范围。 范围听起来很灵活，但实际上它们给研究人员带来了不确定性，并给我们的团队带来了开销。 静态薪酬为双方设定了明确的期望，并且我们保留为超出工作范围的工作酌情颁发奖金的能力。

我们新的公共计划赏金表：

这一调整将使我们能够为我们的 VIP 计划提供更有针对性的关注和更高的奖励，同时仍然使我们的公共计划成为探索和服务 VIP 计划的一个渠道。

提高信号要求

为了减少人工智能生成的省力报告的数量，我们正在公共计划上实施 HackerOne 信号要求。 尚未达到信号阈值的研究人员在建立跟踪记录时将获得有限数量的允许提交。

这并不是阻挡新研究人员的墙。 HackerOne 的平台为未达到门槛的研究人员提供了最多四份初始提交材料，这对于具有真正发现的新人来说有足够的跑道来展示他们的技能。 我们希望继续与整个安全研究社区保持联系；我们只需要一个基线，使该计划对每个人都可行。

什么保持不变

我们对奖励真正的安全研究的承诺没有改变。 我们将继续快速付款，清晰沟通，并将研究人员视为合作伙伴。

在这些更改生效之前提交的报告将按照之前的赏金结构进行兑现。 我们不再考虑积压工作，以便只有 2026 年 7 月 27 日或之后提交的报告才会使用新结构进行评估。

展望未来

这是我们正在经历的更广泛演变的一部分。 除了赏金重组和 VIP 计划之外，我们还投资于更快的响应时间、更清晰的严重性推理和更多的社区参与。 良好的工作关系不仅仅建立在薪酬表上。 您可以在 DEFCON 等会议上直接与我们互动，并且您将通过持续的外展收到我们的消息。 我们期待在安全会议上加入研究人员社区，建立关系，并继续探索如何使我们的错误赏金计划成为奖励我们最关心的那种深入、深思熟虑的研究的方法。

安全研究社区是 GitHub 最大的资产之一。 这些变化是为了向他们致敬。 我们希望建立一个项目来吸引我们重视的研究，创造一种体验来反映我们对这项工作的重视程度，并维护研究人员每次提交报告时对我们的信任。

我们会在那里见到你，祝你黑客快乐！

撰写者

产品安全工程师
